design.theme: A volcanic forge chamber where models are compressed and optimized for edge deployment. Molten metal represents floating-point weights; the anvil represents quantization targets. Steam and pressure represent compression. The room glows with heat maps showing per-layer FLOPs and memory usage, guiding artisans to the most impactful optimizations.
design.ml_concept: Model Compression and Quantization for Edge Deployment — covering post-training quantization (GPTQ, AWQ, bitsandbytes), structured/unstructured pruning (magnitude pruning, lottery ticket hypothesis), knowledge distillation (response-based and feature-based), weight sharing and matrix factorization, latency profiling, energy-aware NAS, binary/ternary neural networks, and edge deployment formats (ONNX, TFLite, TensorRT).
design.description: A volcanic chamber of transformation. Raw models enter heavy with parameters; compressed models leave lean enough to run on any device. Eight stations form a compression pipeline: quantize, prune, distill, compress, benchmark, budget, binarize, and package. The heat represents compute — every optimization reduces the fire needed. Steam hisses from the compression press as gigabytes become megabytes. The binary anvil rings with each extreme-quantized layer. The power budget ledger tracks joules per inference like a miser counting coins. Here, intelligence is not about size — it is about efficiency. The fleet sends its NAS designs from the Engine Room, its fine-tuned models from the Workshop, its attention patterns from the Forge — all arrive here to be made small enough to deploy from the Dry-Dock to the edge.
